---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-27"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)

```

```{r}

---
title: "BBC Bias - Reweighting (Full Categories)"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-27"
---
  

library(tidyverse)
library(here)

country_avg_poll <- read.csv(here("output", "country_avg_poll_full.csv"))


names(country_avg_poll)


country_avg_poll <- country_avg_poll %>%
  rename(
    poll_very_left  = "A.very.strong.left.wing.bias",
    poll_mod_left   = "A.moderate.left.wing.bias",
    poll_no_bias    = "No.left.or.right.wing.bias",
    poll_mod_right  = "A.moderate.right.wing.bias",
    poll_very_right = "A.very.strong.right.wing.bias",
    poll_dk         = "Don.t.know"
  )

# Load constituency geography data
aux <- read.csv("https://mrp-psf.ams3.digitaloceanspaces.com/other-files/const_data_full_new.csv")
aux <- aux %>%
  mutate(const_name = gsub("Ynys Mon", "Ynys Môn", const_name)) %>%
  mutate(
    region_2 = case_when(
      region_name %in% c(
        "East Midlands", "East of England", "Greater London",
        "North East England", "North West England", "South East England",
        "South West England", "West Midlands", "Yorkshire and the Humber",
        "London", "England"
      ) ~ "England",
      region_name == "Scotland" ~ "Scotland",
      region_name == "Wales"    ~ "Wales",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# Load constituency-level MRP predictions (full categories)
preds_bbc <- read.csv(here("output", "bbc_bias_constituency_summary_revised.csv"))



preds_bbc <- bbc_1 %>%
  mutate(
    any_bias = very_left + mod_left + mod_right + very_right,
    no_bias_or_dk = no_bias + dont_know
  )




names(preds_bbc)

# Join region onto predictions
preds_bbc <- preds_bbc %>%
  left_join(aux %>% select(const_name, region_2), by = "const_name") %>%
  mutate(region_2 = case_when(
    const_name == "Ynys MÃ´n" ~ "Wales",
    TRUE ~ region_2
  ))

# Join poll country averages
preds_bbc <- preds_bbc %>%
  left_join(
    country_avg_poll %>% select(country, poll_very_left, poll_mod_left,
                                poll_no_bias, poll_mod_right, poll_very_right, poll_dk),
    by = c("region_2" = "country")
  )

# Compute MRP country averages for each category
preds_bbc <- preds_bbc %>%
  group_by(region_2) %>%
  mutate(
    mrp_avg_very_left  = mean(very_left,  na.rm = TRUE),
    mrp_avg_mod_left   = mean(mod_left,   na.rm = TRUE),
    mrp_avg_no_bias    = mean(no_bias,    na.rm = TRUE),
    mrp_avg_mod_right  = mean(mod_right,  na.rm = TRUE),
    mrp_avg_very_right = mean(very_right, na.rm = TRUE),
    mrp_avg_dk         = mean(dont_know,  na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Apply additive adjustment to each category
##The additive adjustment is preferred over a multiplier approach for reweighting constituency-level BBC bias estimates. Because the categories — very left, moderate left, no bias, don't know, moderate right, very right — are proportions that must collectively sum to 100%, a multiplier approach risks pushing individual category estimates outside plausible bounds, particularly for smaller categories like very strong left or right wing bias where even a modest multiplier could produce implausibly large values. The additive method instead applies a uniform shift to each category based on the gap between the poll and MRP country averages, preserving the relative differences between constituencies while keeping estimates within a realistic range.





preds_bbc_reweighted <- preds_bbc %>%
  mutate(
    very_left_rw  = very_left  + (poll_very_left  - mrp_avg_very_left),
    mod_left_rw   = mod_left   + (poll_mod_left   - mrp_avg_mod_left),
    no_bias_rw    = no_bias    + (poll_no_bias    - mrp_avg_no_bias),
    mod_right_rw  = mod_right  + (poll_mod_right  - mrp_avg_mod_right),
    very_right_rw = very_right + (poll_very_right - mrp_avg_very_right),
    dk_rw         = dont_know  + (poll_dk         - mrp_avg_dk),
    net_rw        = (very_left_rw + mod_left_rw) - (very_right_rw + mod_right_rw)
  ) %>%
  select(const_name, region_2, very_left_rw, mod_left_rw, no_bias_rw,
         mod_right_rw, very_right_rw, dk_rw, net_rw)

# Sanity check - reweighted country averages should match poll averages
preds_bbc %>%
  group_by(region_2) %>%
  summarise(
    mrp_very_left        = mean(very_left),
    reweighted_very_left = mean(very_left + (poll_very_left - mrp_avg_very_left)),
    poll_very_left       = first(poll_very_left)
  )

write.csv(preds_bbc_reweighted, here("output", "preds_bbc_reweighted_full.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```
